text:
Once there was a little old oak tree that lived in the heart of an orange grove. It wasn't that the old tree was particularly vibrant; its leaves had turned that faded autumn gold, its branches stretched out towards the azure sky. But somehow, wherever it went, creatures seemed to gather around it.

One sunny afternoon, a curious rabbit scuttled onto its boughs. The oak tree waved a gentle trunk leaves like curtains to drape its shade over the little visitor.

"Hello there," chirped the rabbit. "Look at all these leaves! I could make blankets!"

With a rustle that sounded almost like laughter, the tree invited the rabbit to nestle down inside its branches. The rabbit nibbled on some of the woodiest leaves, feeling quite comfortable up here. It began telling the tree tales of far-off places.

After just a while, a woodpecker drummed a rhythm into a snag nearby. "That's the call of the nocturnal owl,"
